mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate wage income and consumption expenditures, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above two percent reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises equity discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive sixty-day returns in more than fifty percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds two percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real retail sales and a decline in the unemployment rate jointly signal robust consumer demand and labor market health, amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days beyond the marginal effect of either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term does not improve directional accuracy by at least five percentage points over a three-year window compared to a model with only main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during early-cycle recoveries predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days because rising factory output signals accelerating earnings momentum before capacity constraints bind.
failure_condition: In identified early-cycle periods, industrial production growth fails to exhibit a positive Rank IC over any two-year rolling window, or the hit rate of positive five-day returns falls below fifty-five percent.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative unemployment rate increase exceeding forty basis points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of instances when the unemployment rate trigger is active, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price inflation and a high federal funds rate compress real corporate earnings and increase equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days through a joint effect stronger than either variable alone.
failure_condition: The two-factor interaction model exhibits lower directional accuracy than the best univariate model over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding ten percent from a six-month peak signals weakening residential construction and related durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 sixty-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of instances when the housing starts decline trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding one-half percent indicates above-trend aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days via upward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 five-day returns are not significantly higher than zero during above-trend GDP growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of positive returns falls below fifty-five percent.
